stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 25 subscribers. The sample mean is 264.1 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 65.6 Mbps. However, 3 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 22 observations have a mean of 256.6 Mbps and standard deviation of 49.4 Mbps. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 264.1, 95% CI = [237.0, 291.2]. Without outliers: mean = 256.6, 95% CI = [235.5, 277.7]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [235.5, 291.2]. Point estimate: 260.4 Mbps.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [235.5, 291.2].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
